title: Foundation Governance Record Frontmatter (Stage 1)
description: Draft 2020-12 schema for the YAML frontmatter of every governance record (PRD, PDR, ADR, SPEC). Validates shape; the CI validator (see validator_spec) enforces the cross-record invariants JSON Schema alone cannot express (ID uniqueness, reference resolution, parent-TYPE contract).
$comment: unevaluatedProperties:false (not additionalProperties) is required so the allOf/if-then branches below can legally introduce branch-specific keys. additionalProperties:false would reject them because it is evaluated per-subschema and does not see sibling allOf properties.

id.description: Globally unique record ID. Per-type regex enforced in the type branches below. The FOUND- namespace prefix is reserved for foundation records; CLIENT- is a later stage and MUST NOT appear in Stage 1.

type.description: Record type discriminator. Stage 1 recognises exactly these four. 'tasks' and 'release' are spine stages tracked outside the record registry and are NOT record types here.

title.description: Human title, sentence-case, no trailing period.

status.description: Lifecycle state. proposed=under owner review; accepted=in force; superseded=replaced by a newer record (see supersedes on the successor); deprecated=intentionally retired, no successor; rejected=proposal an owner declined. ADRs are immutable once accepted: change happens by issuing a successor and flipping the old record to superseded, never by rewriting an accepted ADR's decision.

owner.description: Single accountable domain owner as a GitHub handle. Exactly one owner per record (the CODEOWNERS gate resolves to one accountable reviewer). Group handles (containing '/') are disallowed at Stage 1 to keep accountability singular.

date.description: Authored/last-status-change date, ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). ajv-formats validates this in full mode.

references.description: Up-chain parent references: IDs of records higher on the spine that this record depends on/cites. Every entry MUST resolve to an existing record (validator) and MUST satisfy the parent-TYPE contract (parent_type_matrix). Ordering is not significant; duplicates are disallowed.

supersedes.description: If this record replaces a prior record of the SAME type, its ID. The validator asserts: target exists, target.type == this.type, target.status == 'superseded', and no two live records supersede the same target. null or omitted for original records.

supersededBy.description: Back-pointer set on a superseded record to its successor. Present iff status=='superseded' (enforced in the status branch below and cross-checked by the validator against the successor's supersedes).

allOf.$comment: PRD — root of the spine. No up-chain parent exists, so references MUST be empty. IDs pinned to PRD-NNNN.
PDR — product decision. MUST cite exactly one PRD parent (the business case it realises). No other parent types allowed at Stage 1.
ADR — architecture decision. MUST cite exactly one PRD parent. Immutable once accepted (change via supersession). References array is the single PRD it serves.

SPEC — living capability. MUST cite at least one ADR parent; MAY cite multiple ADRs and additionally PDRs (a capability can realise several decisions). At least one ADR is mandatory; PRD/SPEC parents are disallowed. The 'at least one ADR' rule is a contains-constraint here and re-asserted by the validator against the matrix.
Status coupling: a superseded record MUST carry supersededBy; a non-superseded record MUST NOT.
